logger_audit_changes.change: Replaced LrTasks.execute('logger -t structured-export ...') with LrLogger('StructuredExport') via pcall-require guard. The macOS system logger call was wrong for this context and would have failed silently in older environments.
Added import('LrLogger') and local logger = LrLogger('StructuredExport'). Added logger:info at dialog open and after presentModalDialog result.
No changes needed — already had correct LrLogger('StructuredExport') with logger:enable('logfile'), logger:info for normal events, logger:error for caught failures.
No changes needed — already had correct LrLogger('StructuredExport') and logger:info.
No changes needed — pure recursive walker with no SDK-dependent paths; no events worth logging at this level.
No changes needed — thin LrPrefs adapter; no events worth logging.
No changes needed — pure helper functions, no side effects.
No changes needed — pure data table.
No changes needed — SDK manifest, no logic.

assumptions: LrLogger 'logfile' target writes to ~/Documents/LrClassicLogs/StructuredExport.log — this is the documented Lightroom SDK convention for the logfile target with a named logger.
All modules sharing the logger name 'StructuredExport' are covered by the logger:enable('logfile') call in ExportTask.lua; secondary modules do not need their own enable call.
ExportDialog.lua is LR-only (uses import() throughout) so adding import('LrLogger') is safe and does not affect busted test runs, which do not load ExportDialog.
Metadata.lua uses pcall-require for LrLogger to preserve its dual-environment compatibility (busted + LR); in test runs the logger variable is nil and the guard 'if logger then' prevents any call.
